instruction:
Let $U$ the be upper half plane $U=\{( x, y ) \in\mathbb{R}^{2} | y > 0 \}$ and introduce the Poincaré metric
$$
g=y^{-2} ( d x \otimes d x+d y \otimes d y ).
$$
What is the geodesic equation?

Options:
A. $$ y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{1} {y} [ 3 ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+2 ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
B. $$ y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{1} {y} [ ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+2 ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
C. $$ y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{3} {y} [ ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+ ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
D. $$ y^{\prime\prime}+\frac{1} {y} [ ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+3 ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
E. $$ y^{\prime\prime}+\frac{1} {y} [ 2 ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+ ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
F. $$ y^{\prime\prime}+\frac{1} {y} [ 3 ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+ ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
G. $$ y^{\prime\prime}+\frac{2} {y} [ ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+ ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
H. $$
y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{1} {y} [ ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+3 ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0.
$$
I. $$ y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{2} {y} [ 3 ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+ ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
J. $$ y^{\prime\prime}-\frac{1} {y} [ 2 ( x^{\prime} )^{2}+2 ( y^{\prime} )^{2} ]=0. $$
